_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: FL batch: 2 House reps (HD-119 Porras, HD-120 Mooney) + 3 FL Supreme Court justices (Muniz CJ, Canady [RETIRED 2025], Grosshans). Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; judges scored on opinions/legislative record. High-value catches: Mooney voted NO on HB 1557 (Don't Say Gay) and NOT VOTING on SB 300 (6-week ban) = purple-district deviations; Canady retired off the court Dec 31 2025 (status fix).

juan-carlos-porras@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 (evidence_state). Miami-Dade R, HD-119, took office Nov 2022; running for reelection 2026 (verified Ballotpedia). HARD votes: YES SB 300 (6-week abortion ban, 2023); co-introducer + supported HB 1 (2023 universal ESA school choice; the Freedom Index 'NO' is contradicted by the official record where no House R opposed); YES HB 3 (2023 anti-ESG); YES SB 214 (2A Financial Privacy Act, resisting federal firearm-tracking); YES SB 1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary incl. E-Verify mandate); YES HB 1451 (2024 deny IDs to illegal migrants); YES HB 543 (2023 constitutional carry). No confirmed cross-party deviation. Marriage/sexuality individual roll calls and church affiliation not isolated -> null. Note: 6-week ban is a restriction, not abolition, so sanctity q1 (abolition) left null.

0.note: Co-introducer of HB 1 (2023), Florida's universal ESA school-choice expansion; no House Republicans opposed.

4.note: Voted YES on HB 3 (2023), Florida's anti-ESG act, plus SB 56 (2025) rejecting UN climate intervention.

0.note: No isolated Porras-specific roll call on hand-counted ballots.

1.note: No isolated Porras-specific roll call on voter ID.

3.note: No isolated Porras-specific roll call on mail-in/drop boxes.

4.note: No isolated Porras-specific roll call on private election funding.

2.note: Voted YES on SB 214 (2023), the Second Amendment Financial Privacy Act, resisting federal/financial firearm tracking.
Voted YES on SB 1718 (2023), DeSantis anti-sanctuary/immigration enforcement law (did not join the lone GOP defector).

3.note: SB 1718 (2023), which he supported, mandates E-Verify for private employers with 25+ employees.

0.note: Voted YES on HB 543 (2023), permitless/constitutional carry.

jim-mooney-jr@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 (evidence_state). Islamorada/Florida Keys R, HD-120; 3rd term, running 2026 (verified Ballotpedia). Mostly reliable conservative votes (YES HB 5 15-week ban 2022; YES HB 543 constitutional carry 2023; YES HB 1 universal ESA 2023; YES HB 3 minor age-verification 2024; YES SB 1718 anti-sanctuary 2023; YES SB 7050 elections 2023) but TWO documented purple-district DEVIATIONS confirmed against official roll calls: voted NO on HB 1557 (2022 'Parental Rights in Education'/'Don't Say Gay'), and was recorded NOT VOTING (abstained, 1 of 9) on SB 300 (2023 six-week abortion ban) -- he is NOT a 'no' vote on SB 300, he abstained. A third-party aggregator (freedomindex.us) mis-lists his HB 1557 vote as YES; the official FL Senate roll-call PDF is authoritative. Church affiliation not documented -> null.

4.note: Voted NO on HB 1557 (2022, 'Parental Rights in Education'/'Don't Say Gay'), which restricted classroom instruction on sexual orientation/gender identity -- a documented break from the conservative position (confirmed in the official FL Senate roll-call PDF).

0.note: Voted YES on HB 1 (2023), Florida's universal ESA school-choice expansion.

3.note: Voted YES on HB 3 (2024), Online Protections for Minors -- age verification on pornographic/social-media content (119-0).

2.note: Voted YES on SB 1718 (2023), DeSantis anti-sanctuary/immigration enforcement law.

3.note: SB 1718 (2023), which he supported, mandates E-Verify for private employers with 25+ employees.

0.note: Voted YES on HB 543 (2023), permitless/constitutional carry.

carlos-g-muñiz@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 (evidence_federal; engine tiers FL Supreme Court justices as federal). Chief Justice (term as chief ends 2026-06-30; John Couriel elected next chief effective 2026-07-01, Muniz reverts to associate justice). Appointed by DeSantis 2019; textualist/Federalist Society. Pro-life judicial record: joined the 6-1 majority in Planned Parenthood v. State (SC2022-1050, Apr 1 2024) holding the FL Privacy Clause does not protect abortion and receding from prior precedent, upholding the 15-week ban; his concurrence in the Amendment 4 ballot case described the unborn as 'an entire class of human beings' deserving legal protection and pressed at oral argument whether constitutional 'natural person' includes the unborn. NOTE: his vote to place Amendment 4 on the ballot was a judicial-restraint vote, NOT a pro-abortion vote -- do not mis-score. Authored the 2025 majority upholding the DeSantis congressional map against a racial-gerrymander challenge (judicial restraint; does not map to the election-integrity ballot-mechanics cells -> null). Catholic; family supports a pro-life pregnancy center. Next merit-retention vote 2026. No contrary (anti-rubric) evidence found; most categories null (no judicial record).

0.note: His Amendment 4 concurrence describes the unborn as 'an entire class of human beings' deserving legal protection; pressed at oral argument whether constitutional 'natural person' includes the unborn -- an affirmative personhood-leaning judicial statement.

1.note: Joined the 6-1 majority (Planned Parenthood v. State, 2024) holding the FL Constitution does not protect abortion and receding from prior precedent -- a judicial act removing the constitutional shield on abortion, beyond mere restriction.

set.office: Former Associate Justice, Florida Supreme Court (retired Dec 31, 2025)

charles-t-canady@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 (evidence_federal). STATUS FIX: RETIRED from the Florida Supreme Court effective Dec 31, 2025; succeeded by Adam Tanenbaum (assumed office Jan 15, 2026) -- the prior 'sitting Associate Justice / Next Election 2028' record was STALE. Exceptionally strong pro-life record: as U.S. Rep (R-FL, 1993-2001) he authored the federal Partial-Birth Abortion Ban Act (H.R. 1833, 104th Cong.) and is credited with coining 'partial-birth abortion'; on the bench he joined the 6-1 majority in Planned Parenthood v. State (2024) ending FL's constitutional abortion right. As House Judiciary Subcommittee on the Constitution chairman he was central to the Defense of Marriage Act (DOMA, 1996). Received the ERLC (Southern Baptist) John Leland Religious Liberty Award (2000). Properly RECUSED from the FL congressional redistricting case (wife Rep. Jennifer Canady serves in the Legislature) -> no election-integrity score. Federalist Society textualist. Denomination on the prior profile ('Southern Baptist') could not be primary-verified; corrected to denomination-unverified.

1.note: Authored the federal Partial-Birth Abortion Ban Act (coined 'partial-birth abortion') and joined the 6-1 FL majority (2024) removing the constitutional abortion right -- documented lifelong affirmative pro-life advocacy beyond mere restriction.

0.note: As House Judiciary Subcommittee on the Constitution chairman, central to the Defense of Marriage Act (DOMA, 1996) defining marriage as one man and one woman in federal law.

1.note: Championed DOMA, which barred federal recognition of same-sex marriage -- documented opposition to same-sex marriage in law.

profile.religion: Christian (Christian Legal Society member; denomination undocumented)

jamie-r-grosshans@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 (evidence_federal). Associate Justice, appointed by DeSantis 2020; Federalist Society (former Orlando chapter officer), Alliance Defending Freedom Blackstone Fellow, Christian Legal Society member. Strong pro-life judicial record: AUTHORED the 6-1 majority in Planned Parenthood v. State (SC2022-1050, Apr 1 2024) holding the FL Privacy Clause does not protect abortion and receding from In re T.W.; AUTHORED the dissent in the companion Amendment 4 case arguing to keep the abortion amendment OFF the ballot (the misleading-summary dissent). Pre-bench she worked with Operation Outcry aiding a minor whose parents tried to force an abortion. Joined the 2025 majority upholding the DeSantis congressional map. Retained 2022 (~63.8%); next retention 2028. Marriage/sexuality, 2A, and school-choice signed opinions: NO EVIDENCE -> null. Specific denomination undocumented.

1.note: Authored the 6-1 majority (2024) holding the FL Constitution does not protect abortion (receding from In re T.W.) and authored the dissent to keep Amendment 4 off the ballot -- documented affirmative pro-life judicial leadership beyond mere restriction.
